page: Microsoft Azure E128-64ds_v6 is an Edsv6 family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This server features an x86_64 architecture with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 1024.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. It includes 7559 GB of local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

The hardware configuration lacks dedicated graphics processing units, making it unsuitable for hardware-accelerated rendering or deep learning workloads. However, the high density of memory relative to compute cores provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require proportional scaling of CPU licenses. The inclusion of a large 7559 GB local storage volume reduces the immediate need for external block storage attachments for temporary data.

This server type is designed for memory-intensive applications, large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems, and enterprise resource planning software. The combination of high memory capacity and substantial local storage supports data-heavy processing tasks and relational database management systems.
description: Microsoft Azure E128-64ds_v6 is an Edsv6 family virtual machine built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This server features 64 dedicated vCPUs, 1024.0 GB of system memory, and 7559 GB of local storage. With a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU, the instance is structured to support memory-intensive operations. It supports Premium Storage capabilities but does not include any graphics processing units, representing a clear tradeoff for workloads requiring hardware acceleration. This resource density offers qualitative cost efficiency for applications that demand substantial memory capacity without requiring a proportional increase in compute licensing. The server is designed for large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching systems, and enterprise resource planning workloads.
